name: Cotingidae
description: Cotingas are a diverse family of Neotropical passerines known for their elaborate plumage, courtship displays, and lekking behavior. The family includes some of the most spectacularly ornamented birds in the world.

children.name: Rupicola
children.description: Rupicola is a genus of spectacularly crested cotingas that perform elaborate courtship displays on traditional lek sites.

children.name: Rupicola rupicola
children.description: The Guianan cock-of-the-rock is a brilliant orange bird with a fan-shaped crest that covers its bill. It inhabits rocky gorges and foothill forests of the Guiana Shield.

children.name: Rupicola peruvianus
children.description: The Andean cock-of-the-rock is the national bird of Peru, with dazzling scarlet-orange plumage and a prominent crest. Males gather at communal leks where they perform elaborate hopping and wing-flapping displays.

children.name: Procnias
children.description: Procnias is a genus of cotingas known for their extraordinarily loud, bell-like calls that carry for great distances through the forest.

children.name: Procnias nudicollis
children.description: The bare-throated bellbird is entirely white with a bare blue throat patch, found in the Atlantic Forest of Brazil. Its loud, metallic call is one of the most distinctive sounds of the forest.

children.name: Procnias albus
children.description: An entirely white bellbird with a black fleshy wattle, found in the Guiana Shield and northern Amazon. Its call is among the loudest of any bird.

children.name: Procnias averano
children.description: A bellbird with brown upperparts, white underparts, and a distinctive beard-like wattle, found in northern South America.

children.name: Procnias tricarunculatus
children.description: A chestnut-brown bellbird with a white head and three long black wattles, found in the cloud forests of Costa Rica and Panama.

children.name: Lipaugus
children.description: Lipaugus is a genus of relatively plain-plumaged cotingas known for their powerful, far-carrying songs.

children.name: Lipaugus vociferans
children.description: The screaming piha is a drab grey bird with one of the loudest songs of any bird, audible for over a kilometer. It is a characteristic voice of Amazonian lowland forests.

children.name: Lipaugus fuscocinereus
children.description: The dusky piha is a dark grey bird with a slightly paler belly, found in the cloud forests of the Andes from Colombia to Bolivia. Its song is a loud, far-carrying series of whistled notes. It often perches on exposed branches in the canopy.

children.name: Lipaugus streptophorus
children.description: The rose-collared piha is a grey bird with a distinctive rose-pink collar across the upper breast. It is found in the tepui region of Venezuela, Guyana, and northern Brazil. Its song is a series of loud, ringing whistles.

children.name: Lipaugus unirufus
children.description: A rich rufous piha found in lowland rainforests of Central America from Mexico to Panama.

children.name: Lipaugus uropygialis
children.description: A grey piha with rufous rump and modified wing feathers, found in the cloud forests of the Andes.

children.name: Lipaugus conditus
children.description: A plain grey cotinga restricted to the Atlantic Forest of southeastern Brazil, considered vulnerable.

children.name: Lipaugus weberi
children.description: A grey piha with a chestnut cap, found in the Colombian Andes. Critically endangered.

children.name: Lipaugus ater
children.description: A striking cotinga with glossy black upperparts and golden-yellow underparts, from the Atlantic Forest of Brazil.

children.name: Lipaugus unirufus
children.description: A rufous piha from Central America.

children.name: Lipaugus uropygialis
children.description: A piha from the Andes.

children.name: Lipaugus conditus
children.description: A cotinga from the Atlantic Forest of Brazil.

children.name: Lipaugus weberi
children.description: A piha from the Colombian Andes.

children.name: Querula
children.description: Querula is a monotypic genus of cotinga with a unique purple throat patch in males.

children.name: Querula purpurata
children.description: The purple-throated fruitcrow is a glossy black bird with a brilliant purple-red throat patch, found across Central and Amazonian South America. It is highly social and often seen in small groups.

children.name: Cephalopterus
children.description: Cephalopterus is a genus of large cotingas with a distinctive umbrella-like crest and an inflatable chest wattle.

children.name: Cephalopterus ornatus
children.description: The Amazonian umbrellabird is a large, all-black bird with a massive umbrella-shaped crest and a long, feathered wattle on its chest. It inhabits riverine and floodplain forests of the Amazon basin.

children.name: Cephalopterus glabricollis
children.description: A large black cotinga with retractable crest and bare red throat patch, found in Costa Rica and Panama.

children.name: Cephalopterus penduliger
children.description: A large black cotinga with umbrella crest and long pendulous wattle, found in Colombia and Ecuador.

children.name: Cotinga
children.description: A genus of brilliantly coloured cotingas with iridescent blue, purple, or turquoise plumage. Males are among the most dazzlingly coloured birds in the Neotropics, while females are dull brown. They are frugivorous and inhabit the canopy of lowland rainforests.

children.name: Cotinga cayana
children.description: The spangled cotinga has a brilliant turquoise-blue body with a purple throat and black wings spotted with white. It is found in the canopy of lowland rainforests across the Amazon basin. Its call is a thin, high-pitched whistle.

children.name: Cotinga maculata
children.description: The banded cotinga is a brilliant blue cotinga with a band of purple across the breast, endemic to the Atlantic Forest of eastern Brazil. It is considered endangered due to habitat loss. Males have a stunning iridescent plumage that shimmers in the canopy light.

children.name: Cotinga cotinga
children.description: The purple-breasted cotinga has a deep violet-blue body with a bright purple breast patch and black wings. It is found in the lowland rainforests of the Guiana Shield and the northern Amazon. It feeds on fruit in the canopy, often in small groups.

children.name: Cotinga maynana
children.description: The plum-throated cotinga has a turquoise body with a deep plum-coloured throat patch and black wings. It is found in the western Amazon basin from Colombia to Bolivia. It inhabits the canopy of lowland rainforests and feeds on fruit.

children.name: Cotinga amabilis
children.description: The lovely cotinga has a brilliant turquoise body, a violet throat, and black wings with white edges. It is found from southern Mexico to Costa Rica in the canopy of lowland rainforests. Its call is a thin, sibilant whistle.

children.name: Cotinga ridgwayi
children.description: The turquoise cotinga is a stunning blue-green bird with a violet throat, found in the Pacific lowlands of Costa Rica and Panama. It inhabits humid lowland forests and is considered near threatened. Its diet consists primarily of fruit.

children.name: Cotinga nattererii
children.description: A brilliant turquoise-blue cotinga with violet throat, found from Panama to northwestern South America.

children.name: Carpodectes
children.description: A genus of dazzling white cotingas found in Central America. Males are predominantly white with subtle grey or yellow tinges on the body, making them among the most striking birds in the Neotropics.

children.name: Carpodectes nitidus
children.description: The snowy cotinga is a pure white bird with a pale bluish-grey tinge on the wings and tail, found in the lowland forests of Costa Rica, Panama, and western Colombia. It is one of the whitest of all birds. It feeds on fruit in the canopy.

children.name: Carpodectes antoniae
children.description: The yellow-billed cotinga is a white bird with a distinctive pale yellow bill and greyish wings, found in the Pacific lowlands of Costa Rica and Panama. It is considered endangered due to habitat loss. Its call is a soft, whistled note.

children.name: Calyptura
children.description: A monotypic genus of tiny, colourful cotinga that was once thought extinct. It is one of the smallest members of the family and was rediscovered in the 1990s after decades without records.

children.name: Calyptura cristata
children.description: The kinglet calyptura is a tiny bird, only 8 cm long, with a bright yellow body, olive wings, and a distinctive red-and-yellow crown patch. It is endemic to the Atlantic Forest of southeastern Brazil. Once thought extinct, it was rediscovered in 1996 after a 90-year absence of confirmed records.

children.name: Xipholena
children.description: A genus of spectacular cotingas with the males in brilliant purple or wine-red plumage and contrasting white wings.

children.name: Xipholena punicea
children.description: A stunning cotinga with the male brilliant wine-red with white wings, found across the Amazon basin.

children.name: Xipholena atropurpurea
children.description: A cotinga with deep purple-red body and white wings, endemic to the Atlantic Forest of Brazil. Endangered.

children.name: Xipholena lamellipennis
children.description: A spectacular cotinga with glossy purple body and white tail, found in the Guiana Shield.

children.name: Phoenicircus
children.description: A genus of spectacular brilliant-red cotingas found in the Amazon basin and Guiana Shield.

children.name: Phoenicircus carnifex
children.description: A brilliant crimson cotinga with black-edged wings, found in the Guiana Shield.

children.name: Phoenicircus nigricollis
children.description: A brilliant crimson cotinga with a black throat, found in the western Amazon basin.

children.name: Iodopleura
children.description: Tiny cotingas with distinctive purple tufts of feathers on the flanks, found in the Amazon basin.

children.name: Iodopleura pipra
children.description: A tiny cotinga with violet upperparts and purple flank tufts, found in the Amazon basin.

children.name: Iodopleura leucopygia
children.description: A tiny cotinga with violet upperparts and white rump, found in the Guiana Shield.

children.name: Iodopleura isabellae
children.description: A tiny cotinga with brown upperparts and white eyebrow, found in the western Amazon.

children.name: Tijuca
children.description: A genus of cotingas endemic to the Atlantic Forest of Brazil, with striking black-and-gold or grey plumage.

children.name: Tijuca atra
children.description: A striking cotinga with glossy black upperparts and golden-yellow belly, endemic to the Atlantic Forest.

children.name: Tijuca condita
children.description: A plain grey cotinga restricted to the Atlantic Forest of Brazil, discovered in 1982.

children.name: Carpornis
children.description: A genus of medium-sized cotingas with hooded heads, endemic to the Atlantic Forest of Brazil.

children.name: Carpornis cucullata
children.description: A cotinga with olive-green upperparts, black hood, and yellow underparts, endemic to the Atlantic Forest.

children.name: Carpornis melanocephala
children.description: A cotinga with olive-green upperparts, black head, and bright yellow underparts, endemic to the Atlantic Forest.

children.name: Snowornis
children.description: A genus of dull-plumaged pihas found in the cloud forests of the Andes.

children.name: Snowornis cryptolophus
children.description: A dull olive-green piha with a pale belly, found in the cloud forests of the Andes.

children.name: Snowornis subalaris
children.description: An olive-green piha with a distinctive grey tail, found in the cloud forests of the Andes.

children.name: Porphyrolaema
children.description: A monotypic genus of stunning cotinga with a brilliant purple throat.

children.name: Porphyrolaema porphyrolaema
children.description: A stunning cotinga with brilliant purple throat, white underparts, and black upperparts with blue shoulders, from the western Amazon.

children.name: Pyroderus
children.description: A monotypic genus of massive black fruitcrow with a brilliant red throat patch.

children.name: Pyroderus scutatus
children.description: A massive black cotinga with a brilliant red-orange throat patch, found from Colombia to Argentina.

children.name: Haematoderus
children.description: A monotypic genus of spectacular all-red fruitcrow.

children.name: Haematoderus militaris
children.description: A spectacular cotinga with the male entirely crimson-red, found in the Guiana Shield and northern Brazil.

children.name: Gymnoderus
children.description: A monotypic genus of black fruitcrow with bare blue facial skin.

children.name: Gymnoderus foetidus
children.description: A large cotinga with the male entirely black with bare blue facial skin, found in the Amazon basin.

children.name: Perissocephalus
children.description: A monotypic genus of bizarre cotinga with a distinctive mooing call.

children.name: Perissocephalus tricolor
children.description: A bizarre cotinga with bare blue-grey face, black cap, and rich brown body. Its far-carrying mooing call is distinctive in the Guiana Shield.

children.name: Pipreola
children.description: A genus of colourful, fruit-eating cotingas found in the cloud forests of the Andes. Males are brilliantly patterned with green, red, yellow, and black.

children.name: Pipreola chlorolepidota
children.description: A fruiteater with green upperparts and brilliant orange throat, found in Colombia and Ecuador.

children.name: Pipreola frontalis
children.description: A fruiteater with green upperparts and scarlet breast band, found from Colombia to Bolivia.

children.name: Pipreola lubomirskii
children.description: A fruiteater with green upperparts and black breast band, found from Colombia to Peru.

children.name: Pipreola pulchra
children.description: A fruiteater with green upperparts and black face mask, endemic to the montane forests of Peru.

children.name: Pipreola aureopectus
children.description: A stunning fruiteater with green upperparts and golden-yellow breast, found from Colombia to Bolivia.

children.name: Pipreola jucunda
children.description: A fruiteater with green upperparts and bright orange breast, found in Colombia and Ecuador.

children.name: Pipreola arcuata
children.description: A striking fruiteater with green upperparts and barred black-and-yellow underparts, found from Colombia to Bolivia.

children.name: Pipreola intermedia
children.description: A fruiteater with green upperparts and chestnut nape, found from Peru to Bolivia.

children.name: Pipreola riefferii
children.description: A fruiteater with green upperparts and barred black-and-yellow underparts, found from Colombia to Peru.

children.name: Ampelioides
children.description: A monotypic genus of fruiteater with distinctive scaled underparts.

children.name: Ampelioides tschudii
children.description: A distinctive fruiteater with green upperparts and bold black scaling on the yellow underparts, found in the Andes.

children.name: Phytotoma
children.description: A genus of cotingas with short stout bills adapted for feeding on leaves and buds, found in southern South America.

children.name: Phytotoma raimondii
children.description: A cotinga with grey upperparts and short stout bill, endemic to the dry forests of northwestern Peru. Endangered.

children.name: Phytotoma rutila
children.description: A cotinga with white wing patches, found from Bolivia to Argentina.

children.name: Phytotoma rara
children.description: A cotinga with brown upperparts and rufous tail, found in Chile and Argentina.

children.name: Zaratornis
children.description: A monotypic genus restricted to high-altitude Polylepis forests of the Peruvian Andes.

children.name: Zaratornis stresemanni
children.description: A grey-brown cotinga with white cheeks, found in high-altitude Polylepis forests of Peru.

children.name: Conioptilon
children.description: A monotypic genus of cotinga with a distinctive black face, found in the western Amazon.

children.name: Conioptilon mcilhennyi
children.description: A distinctive cotinga with a black face and throat, grey body, and black wings, found in the western Amazon.
